papers.title: Attention Is All You Need
papers.summary: Introduces the Transformer architecture, replacing recurrent sequence modeling with self-attention and feed-forward blocks. It is still the conceptual base layer for most modern language, vision, and multimodal models.
papers.reason: Transformer fundamentals; worth revisiting whenever reading efficient attention, long-context, or agent papers.

papers.title: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models
papers.summary: Shows that large models can be adapted by training low-rank update matrices while keeping the original weights frozen. The method became a practical default for lightweight fine-tuning and personalization.
papers.reason: Small-footprint adaptation is a recurring theme in current applied LLM work.

papers.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
papers.summary: Combines parametric generation with non-parametric retrieval so models can condition on external evidence. It remains a useful reference point for modern RAG, tool-use, and knowledge grounding systems.
papers.reason: A clean anchor paper for reading newer retrieval, memory, and agentic search systems.

papers.title: Segment Anything
papers.summary: Presents a promptable segmentation model and a large-scale segmentation dataset. The paper helped move segmentation toward reusable foundation-model style interfaces.
papers.reason: Useful baseline for promptable vision systems, annotation tools, and visual foundation models.

papers.title: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision
papers.summary: Trains image representations from image-text pairs and demonstrates strong zero-shot transfer. The paper is a foundational reference for multimodal retrieval, classification, and representation learning.
papers.reason: A compact bridge between vision, language, retrieval, and zero-shot evaluation.

papers.title: DINOv2: Learning Robust Visual Features without Supervision
papers.summary: Develops strong self-supervised visual features that transfer across many downstream tasks. It is a practical reference for representation quality without hand-labeled supervision.
papers.reason: Strong comparison point for visual embeddings, retrieval, and dense prediction papers.

papers.title: RT-2: Vision-Language-Action Models Transfer Web Knowledge to Robotic Control
papers.summary: Connects vision-language pretraining with robotic action prediction. The work is useful for understanding how web-scale semantic knowledge can transfer into embodied control.
papers.reason: Good entry point for multimodal robotics and action-conditioned model design.

papers.title: Mamba: Linear-Time Sequence Modeling with Selective State Spaces
papers.summary: Introduces a selective state space architecture aimed at efficient long-sequence modeling. It is often discussed alongside attention alternatives and hardware-aware sequence model design.
papers.reason: Worth tracking when long context, inference throughput, and model architecture tradeoffs matter.
